title: camelina sativa subsp. rumelica
intro: Camelina sativa subsp. rumelica, also known as the Balkan false flax, is a subspecies of the Camelina sativa plant, a flowering annual plant native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of omega-3 fatty acids, antioxidants, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Camelina sativa subsp. rumelica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support brain function. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of oils, capsules, and teas, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
